data_sources.name: CDC Tick Bite Data Tracker (ER Visits for Tick Bites)
data_sources.description: National syndromic surveillance of emergency department visits for tick bites — by week, month, region, age, sex. Best real-time proxy for disease burden. 2026 spring data: highest ER visit rates since 2017 in Northeast + Midwest.
data_sources.notes: Excellent proxy for overall tick exposure/burden, especially where case reporting is low.

data_sources.name: Alpha-gal IgE Prevalence in U.S. Military Recruits (2024 study)
data_sources.description: 6.0% overall positivity; highest in AR (39%), OK (35%), MO (29%). Closely tracks Lone Star tick range. Strong South burden indicator.
data_sources.notes: Undercounts clinical cases but shows massive exposure in the South that traditional Lyme surveillance misses.

data_sources.name: AGS Healthcare Utilization Studies (Anaphylaxis & ER/Hospital Burden)
data_sources.description: Multiple studies show >50% of AGS patients experience anaphylaxis requiring ED visits (some multiple hospitalizations). Delayed diagnosis drives high costs. Underdiagnosed AGS in South/Midwest leads to repeated ER visits.
data_sources.notes: Direct support for disease burden / healthcare cost layer.

notes: Most other items in this dump (Arkansas/Missouri AGS numbers, MMWR, California/Oregon/Washington) were already integrated from prior batches. This batch adds strong burden/proxy data (ER visits + seroprevalence + utilization) that directly supports the red layer and regional storytelling (South = AGS-heavy burden).
